{"key_event":"Militia cantonization and foreign occupation","reasoning":"By 1987 the Lebanese state had lost effective sovereignty over large areas, with the army fragmented, fiscal capacity captured by militias, and order often imposed by Syria or Israel rather than Beirut. Society remained highly mobilized through sectarian parties, militias, unions, communal networks, and resistance groups, but cross-sectarian civic life and independent accountability were badly damaged by displacement, war economies, proxy control, and violence.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":6.8,"state_change":-1.2,"state_power":1.8}
